residual_risks: AboutView mailto row is silently hidden if URL(string: "mailto:\(appSupportMail)") returns nil. This is strictly safer than the previous force-unwrap (which would have crashed), but if AppSupportMail is ever configured to a value with URL-illegal characters (spaces, unencoded angle brackets, etc.) the entire 'App email' row disappears with no user-visible error. The build-time ENV var APP_SUPPORT_MAIL is controlled internally so this is low risk in practice. Confidence too low to report as finding.

URL(staticString:) uses '\(staticString)' interpolation which converts StaticString to String at runtime. Verified in a local Swift REPL that this preserves the exact literal contents for typical URL strings (including URLs with fragments like '#faq' and path components). The single preconditionFailure is reachable only if someone passes a literally malformed URL — but StaticString only accepts compile-time literals, so this is caught in dev. The author explicitly blessed this pattern; not a finding.

static let URL(staticString:) constants are lazy in Swift, so a malformed literal would only crash on first access of that specific constant. All four converted URLs (paypal.me/creepymonstr, github.com/sponsors/CinimoDY, github.com/.../FAQ.md#faq, github.com/CinimoDY/DOSBTS) parse successfully via URL(string:) — no crash risk.

Library/Extensions/URL.swift is under Library/ (fileSystemSynchronized) and is NOT in the widget's exclusion set (which still only excludes Extensions/Float.swift + Resources/*). The extension imports only Foundation, so widget-target compilation works. Confirmed via pbxproj inspection.

DirectConfig.appSchemaURL intentionally remains URL(string: "dosbts://") (Optional<URL>) because it's compared to event.url (Optional<URL>) in AppleCalendarExport.swift:114/185 and assigned at :155. Not converted to URL(staticString:) — this is deliberate and preserves existing semantics.

Removed @available guards in AppleHealthImport.swift (iOS 15.4 on fetchNutritionSamples/fetchExerciseSamples, iOS 16.0 on workout.statistics) are now covered by the iOS 26.0 deployment target. HKSampleQueryDescriptor (iOS 15.4+), HKQuantityType(.dietaryCarbohydrates) shorthand (iOS 15.4+), and HKWorkout.statistics(for:) (iOS 16.0+) are all available at iOS 26.0.
Removed legacy eventStore.requestAccess(to:) branch in AppleCalendarExport.swift. requestFullAccessToEvents (iOS 17.0+) is available at iOS 26.0. No code path depended on the old API.
Removed if #available(iOS 16.1, *) wrapping widgetCenterMiddleware() in App.swift:227,278. Middleware function no longer carries @available attribute, unconditional call is type-correct.

Removed ChartViewCompatibility.swift (deleted file). All private types (TextInfoCompatibility, ZoomLevelCompatibility, MinuteUpdaterCompatibility, private Color | operator) were self-contained. No external references found (verified via grep).
Removed @State showImagePicker in FoodPhotoAnalysisView.swift along with its usage site. No orphan references.
Removed Localizable.strings keys ("App donate", "App facebook group", "App translation") are not referenced anywhere in Swift code (verified via grep). Clean removal.
testing_gaps: No runtime smoke test that validates all four DirectConfig URL(staticString:) constants actually parse (they should — verified statically, but a single-assertion test like #expect(DirectConfig.faqURL.absoluteString.hasPrefix("https://")) would catch future mistakes where someone adds a 5th malformed URL constant).
No test verifies AboutView's mailto row behavior when appSupportMail contains URL-invalid characters (edge case, low priority).

No UI-level verification that the widget extension still compiles and links with the new Library/Extensions/URL.swift included; static analysis says yes (Foundation-only, not in exclusion set), but only xcodebuild on the DOSBTSWidget scheme would confirm.
